title: Vincetoxicum tenuipedunculatum
taxonomy: Vincetoxicum tenuipedunculatum is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as dogbane or oleander dogbane. It belongs to the genus Vincetoxicum, which includes a number of species commonly known as snake flowers or dog's-tooth violets. The plant is native to China and is characterized by its small, greenish-yellow flowers and greenish-yellow seeds. The species is often classified as a type of milkweed, although its exact classification can vary depending on the source. As a member of the Apocynaceae family, Vincetoxicum tenuipedunculatum shares characteristics with other plants in the same family.

intro: Vincetoxicum tenuipedunculatum, also known as Chinese swallowwort, is a species of flowering plant in the Asclepiadaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and snake bites, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of Vincetoxicum tenuipedunculatum include vincetoxicine, vincamine, and other alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs to make various pharmaceutical preparations, including capsules and extracts.
